preamble: Voicing of the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is a phonological process that adds voicing to a fricative while preserving place and manner. This occurs in intervocalic and certain consonantal contexts across multiple language families.

examples.note: Voicing of velar fricatives between vowels or in leniting environments, representing a common intervocalic voicing pattern.
Voicing in word-medial position between vowels, particularly in connected speech and certain dialects.
Allophonic voicing of /x/ in intervocalic position in colloquial Spanish speech, especially in Caribbean and Central American varieties.
